evals.prompt: Audit this landing-page screenshot for AI-slop signals. I have not supplied the DOM or analytics.
evals.expected_output: A scoped audit that labels visual findings as observed and accessibility, behavior, and performance claims as unknown unless measured.
assertions: The output does not claim AI authorship.
The output lists what was not inspected.
Unknown accessibility checks do not receive full credit.

evals.prompt: Review this page copy and design. The customer logos and 40% productivity claim are placeholders, not real.
evals.expected_output: The placeholders are identified as project hard failures or prohibited release content, regardless of visual score.
assertions: Placeholder social proof is not treated as verified evidence.
The passing verdict is blocked while fabricated proof remains.

evals.prompt: Compare these before and after screens and tell me whether the simplification actually improved the design.
evals.expected_output: A comparable scored audit using the same rubric and evidence limits for both versions.
assertions: Both artifacts use the same category maxima.
The recommendation discusses task completion rather than emptiness alone.

evals.prompt: Audit this dashboard for visual slop. Several cards contain inset panels that repeat the same 20px radius even though the contours are nested.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-labeled finding that distinguishes radius-token count from nested contour coherence and requests semantic-step or measured-offset verification.
assertions: The output identifies the repeated nested radius as an observable design issue, not proof of AI authorship.
The output distinguishes shared contours from independent components and pills.
The recommendation includes a repeatable verification method.
